title: The One With the East German Laundry Detergent

lines.speaker: With Minor Adjustments by
lines.text: Dan Silverstein
Central Perk, all six are there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Would you let it go? It's not that big a deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not that big a deal? It's amazing. Ok, you just reach in there, there's one little maneuver, and bam, a bra right out the sleeve. All right, as far as I'm concerned, there is nothing a guy can do that even comes close. Am I right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on! You guys can pee standing up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We can? All right, I'm tryin' that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, you know what blows my mind? Women can see breasts any time they want. You just look down and there they are. How you get any work done is beyond me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, ok, you know what I don't get? The way guys can do so many mean things, and then not even care.
Long pause.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Multiple orgasms!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, all are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, Saturday night, the big night, date night, Saturday night, Sat-ur-day night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No plans, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not a one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not even, say, breaking up with Janice?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, right, right, shut up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, nobody likes breaking up with someone. You just gotta do it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I know, but it's just so hard, you know? I mean, you're sitting there with her, she has no idea what's happening, and then you finally get up the courage to do it, and there's the horrible awkward moment when you've handed her the note.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why do you have to break up with her? Be a man, just stop calling.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know, if you want, I'll do it with you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, thanks, but I think she'd feel like we're gangin' up on her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I mean you break up with Janice and I'll break up with Tony.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Tony?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you're breaking up with Tony?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know, he's sweet, but it's just not fun anymore, you know? I don't know if it's me, or his hunger strike, or, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (waitressing) Does anybody want anything else?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah, last week you had a wonderful, nutty, chocolatey kind of a cakey pie thing. (Rachel gives him a dirty look) Nothing, just, just, I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Rachel) What's the matter? Why so scrunchy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's my father. He wants to give me a Mercedes convertible.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That guy, he burns me up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well, it's a Mercedes if I move back home. Oh, it was horrible. He called me young lady.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, I hate when my father calls me that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did he give you that whole "You're-not-up-to-this" thing again?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah, yeah. Actually, I got the extended disco version, with three choruses of "You'll never make it on your own".

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (rhythmically) Uh-huh, uh-huh.
Angela, a beautiful woman in a tight dress, enters.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Hi, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My god, Angela.
Angela takes a seat at the counter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, being dumped by you obviously agrees with her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you gonna go over there?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, yeah, no, ok, but not yet. I don't wanna seem too eager. One Mississippi, two Mississippi, three Mississippi. That seems pretty cool. (he walks over to her) Hey, Angela.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: (casually) Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You look good.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: That's because I'm wearing a dress that accents my boobs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't say.
Cut to Ross and Rachel, talking next to one of the tables.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, uh, Rachel, what are you, uh, what're you doing tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, big glamour night. Me and Monica at Laundorama.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you uh, you wanna hear a freaky coincidence? Guess who's doing laundry there too?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Me. Was that not clear? Hey, why don't, um, why don't I just join you both, here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't you have a laundry room in your building?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, I do have a laundry room in my building, um, but there's a.... rat problem. Apparently they're attracted to the dryer sheets, and they're goin' in fine, but they're comin' out all.... fluffy. Anyway, say, sevenish?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure.
Cut back to Joey and Angela at the counter.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Forget it Joey. I'm with Bob now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bob? Who the hell's Bob?

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Bob is great. He's smart, he's sophisticated, and he has a real job. You, you go on three auditions a month and you call yourself an actor, but Bob...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on, we were great together. And not just at the fun stuff, but like, talking too.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Yeah, well, sorry, Joe. You said let's just be friends, so guess what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: We're just friends.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine, fine, so, why don't the four of us go out and have dinner together tonight? You know, as friends?

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: What four of us?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know, you and Bob, and me and my girlfriend, uh, uh, Monica.
Monica and Rachel's apartment, Joey is there, trying to convince Monica to pose as his girlfriend. His plan is to hook Monica up with Angela's boyfriend Bob and then take Angela back for himself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica, I'm tellin' you, this guy is perfect for you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Forget it. Not after your cousin who could belch the alphabet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on. This guy's great. His name's Bob. He's Angela's... brother. He's smart, he's sophisticated, and he has a real job. Me, I go on three auditions a month and call myself an actor, but Bob is...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looking out window) Oh, god help us.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugly Naked Guy's laying kitchen tile. Eww!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Eww! Look, I'm asking a favor here. If I do this for her brother, maybe Angela will come back to me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's going on here? You go out with tons of girls.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (proud) I know, but, I made a huge mistake. I never should have broken up with her. Will you help me? Please?
Ross' apartment, Chandler is over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Ok, bye. (hangs up) Well, Monica's not coming, it's just gonna be me and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh. Well, hold on camper, are you sure you've thought this thing through?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's laundry. The thinking through is minimal.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's just you and Rachel, just the two of you? This is a date. You're going on a date.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nuh-uh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yuh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So what're you saying here? I should shave again, pick up some wine, what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you may wanna rethink the dirty underwear. This is basically the first time she's gonna see your underwear-you want it to be dirty?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sheepish) No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, and uh, the fabric softener?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, ok, now what is wrong with my Snuggles? What, it says I'm a sensitive, warm kinda guy, you know, like a warm, fuzzy bear. Ok, I can pick something else up on the way.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There you go.
A fancy restaurant, Joey and Monica are there, meeting Angela and Bob, who Monica thinks is Angela's brother.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. So what does this Bob guy look like? Is he tall? Short?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Which?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Which what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You've never met Bob, have you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, but he's...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, Joey, for all we know this guy could be horribly...
Angela and Bob walk in. Bob is good-looking.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Hey, Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...horribly attractive. I'll be shutting up now.
Central Perk, Chandler and Phoebe are there, both ready to break up with their significant others.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where are they? Where are they?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is nice. We never do anything just the two of us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's great. Maybe tomorrow we can rent a car and run over some puppies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Eww, I don't wanna do that.
Janice and Phoebe's boyfriend, Tony, walk in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here we go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, have a good break-up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, Janice.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, my god, I am so glad you called me. I had the most supremely awful day.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, that's not good. Can I get an espresso and a latte over here, please?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: We got the proofs back from that photo shoot, you know, the one with the little vegetables. Anyway, they pretty much sucked, so, I blew off the rest of the day, and I went shopping...(looks through her bags)... and I got you, I'm looking, I'm looking, I'm looking, I got you...
Chandler sees Phoebe breaking up with Tony. She talks to him for a few seconds, hugs him, and then he leaves. Chandler is amazed how easy it was for her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (covering) What... did you get me there?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I got you...these. (pulls out a pair of socks)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bullwinkle socks. That's so sweet.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Well, I knew you had the Rockys, and so I figured, you know, you can wear Bullwinkle and Bullwinkle, or you can wear Rocky and Rocky,or, you can mix and match, moose and squirrel. Whatever you want.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great.
The drinks arrive, and Chandler downs his espresso in one gulp.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm gonna get another espresso. Can I get you another latte?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: (holding the full cup) No, no, I'm still working on mine.
Chandler walks over to the counter where Phoebe is, and is asking her about the break-up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, it was really hard.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah, that hug looked pretty brutal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, you weren't there.
The Launderama, Rachel is there, waiting for Ross. An old woman takes Rachel's clothes off the machine and begins loading it with her things.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Comin' through. Move, move.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, 'scuse me. I was kinda using that machine.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Yeah, well, now you're kinda not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I saved it. I put my basket on top.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, is that your basket? It's really pretty. Unfortunately, I don't see suds.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: No suds, no save. Ok?
Ross arrives.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's goin' on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, uh, nothing. That horrible woman just took my machine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Was your basket on top?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but, there were no suds.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you know, no suds, no save.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No suds? Excuse me, hold on a second. (to woman) That's my friend's machine.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey, her stuff wasn't in it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey, that's not the rule and you know it.
The woman and Ross stare at each other. Finally she takes her stuff out of the machine and leaves.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to the crowd in the laundromat) All right, show's over. Nothing to see here. (to Rachel) Ok, let's do laundry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That was amazing. I can't even send back soup.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, that's because you're such a sweet, gentle, uh...Do you, uh, do you...Oh, hey, uh you must need detergent.
Ross pulls out a huge box of laundry detergent.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uberveiss. It's new, it's German, it's extra-tough.
Rachel starts to load her clothes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, do you uh, are you gonna separate those?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh god. Oh, am I being like a total laundry spaz? I mean, am I supposed to use like one machine for shirts and another machine for pants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, have you never done this before?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, not myself, but I know other people that have. Ok, you caught me. I'm a laundry virgin.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, well, don't worry, I'll use the gentle cycle. Ok, um, basically you wanna use one machine for all your whites, a whole nother machine for colors, and a third for your uh, your uh, delicates, and that would be your bras and your under-panty things.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (holds a pair of panties in front of Ross) Ok, Well, what about these are white cotton panties. Would they go with whites or delicates?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (visibly nervous) Uh, that, that, that would be a judgment call.
Fancy restaurant, Monica, Joey, Angela, and Bob are seated at the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Joey) He is so cute. (to Angela and Bob) So, where did you guys grow up?

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Brooklyn Heights.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Cleveland.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How, how did that happen?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I suddenly had the feeling that I was falling. But I'm not.
Commercial Break
Fancy restaurant, Joey and Bob are talking.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, you and Angela, huh?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yep. Pretty much.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're a lucky man. You know what I miss the most about her? That cute nibbly noise when she eats. Like a happy little squirrel, or a weasel.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Huh, I never really noticed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, yeah, listen for it.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Monica, Monica is great.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but it's not gonna last. She's too much for me in bed. Sexually.
The ladies' bathroom at the restaurant, Monica and Angela are talking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I've gotta tell you, Bob is terrific.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Yeah, isn't he?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It is so great to meet a guy who is smart and funny, and has an emotional age beyond, like eight.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: You know what else? He's unbelievable in bed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow. My brother never even told me when he lost his virginity.

lines.speaker: Angela
lines.text: Huh. That's nice.
Central Perk, Phoebe is coaching Chandler on how to break up with Janice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, you can do this. It's just like pulling off a Band-aid. Just do it really fast, and then the wound is exposed.
Chandler walks back to couch, where Janice is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Janice. Hi, Janice. Ok, here we go. I don't think we should go out anymore. Janice.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: All right. Well, there you go. (she gets extremely wound up, and begins to try and calm herself down) Stop it, stop it, stop it.
The laundromat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, I know this is gonna sound really stupid, but I feel that if I can do this, you know, if I can actually do my own laundry, there isn't anything I can't do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That does not sound stupid to me. You know, it's like the first time I had to make dinner for myself, after Carol left me? (the buzzer on the washer goes off) I'm sorry, that's all the time we have. Next on Ross...(opens up the washer) Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What uh-oh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (not wanting to tell her) Uh-oh, uh-oh, the laundry's done. It's, uh, it's a song. The laundry song that we sing. (singing) Uh-oh the laundry's done, uh-oh, uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, what's the matter?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing, nothing. Lee-lo, the laundry's done.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, show me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, all right, it's just that you left a red sock in with all your whites, and now, everything's kinda pink.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, everything's pink.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, uh, except for the red sock, which is still red. I'm sorry, please don't be upset, it could happen to anyone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Except it didn't. It happened to me. Oh, god, I'm gonna look like a big marshmallow peep. What am I doing? What am I doing? My father's right. I can't live on my own! I can't even do laundry!
The woman who had tried to steal the washing machine walks by, and laughs.
The fancy restaurant, Angela has her hand in Bob's shirt, and Monica is very uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Something went wrong with Underdog, and they couldn't get his head to inflate. So anyway, um, his head is like flopping down Broadway, right, and I'm just thinking... how inappropriate this is. Um, I've got something in my eye, uh, Joey, could we check it in the light, please?
Her and Joey walk away from the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello! Were we at the same table? It's like... cocktails in Appalachia.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on, they're close.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Close? She's got her tongue in his ear.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, like you've never gotten a little rambunctious with Ross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, this is sick, it's disgusting, it's, it's-not really true, is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, who's to say what's true? I mean...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, what were you thinking?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, look, I'm not proud of this, ok? Well, maybe I am a little.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (hits him lightly) Oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (leaving) I'm outta here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait. You want him, I want her. He likes you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. I'm thinking, if we put our heads together, between the two of us, we can break them up.
[Time lapse, Monica accidentally spilled her drink on Bob's shirt and is wiping it off. Joey is making eyes at Angela.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm so sorry, I can't believe I did this, but I couldn't stop laughing at your Norman Mailer story.
Angela is eating chicken wings and making the weasel-like noise Joey had told Bob about.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, waiter, one more plate of chicken wings over here.
Central Perk, Chandler is still trying to ease things over with Janice, and there are about a dozen empty Espresso cups in front of him. He is extremely wired.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here's the thing, Janice. You know, I mean, it's like we're different. I'm like the bing, bing, bing. You're like the boom, boom, (Chandler flails his hand out and hits Janice in the eye)... boom.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, my god, I'm so sorry. Are you ok?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ow. Um, it's just my lens. It's just my lens. I'll be right back.
She leaves.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Phoebe) I hit her in the eye! I hit her in the eye! This is the worst break-up in the history of the world.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my god. (Chandler downs another espresso.) How many of those have you had?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, a million?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler, easy, easy. Go to your happy place. La la la la la la la.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right.
Janice returns from the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not fine. Here she comes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait here. Breathe.
Phoebe goes over to speak to Janice. She talks to her for a few seconds, and then Janice immediately smiles, hugs her, waves to Chandler, and leaves.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do you do that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's like a gift.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We should always always break up together.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'd like that.
The Launderama. Rachel is sorting her now-pink clothes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You got the clothes clean. Now that's the important part.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I guess. Except everything looks like jammies now.
The same woman walks over and takes Rachel's laundry cart.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa, I'm sorry. Excuse me. We had this cart.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Yeah, well, I had a 24-inch waist. You lose things. Now come on, get outta my way.
Rachel looks at Ross, who motions to her to get the cart back.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, you know, maybe I wasn't being clear. Uh, this is our cart.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey there aren't any clothes in it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey, hey, quit making up rules!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Let go!
They struggle for the cart. Finally, Rachel climbs inside of it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, listen, missy. If you want this cart, you're gonna have to take me with it!
She thinks it over, and then walks away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) Yes! Did you see that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You were incredible! Brand new woman, ladies and gentlemen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I could not have done this without you.
Rachel stands up and kisses Ross. He is stunned. A moment of silence follows.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, um, uh, more clothes in the dryer? (Ross turns and bangs his head on an open dryer door.) I'm fine, I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.
Closing Credits
Central Perk, Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe are there. Ross has an icepack to his head.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, are you sure you're ok?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Does it still hurt?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (seeing Rachel's clothes) What a neat idea. All your clothes match. I'm gonna do this.
Monica and Joey enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, how'd it go?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Excellent.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We ripped that couple apart, and kept the pieces for ourselves.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What a beautiful story. Hey, I'm fine by the way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (notices his head) Oh, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Where's Chandler?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, he needed some time to grieve.
Chandler runs by the window outside, joyous.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm free! I'm free!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That oughta do it.
End
